steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes the simulation's result, which must be 'consistent with previous findings.' The previous finding: narrow-range species are more genetically homogeneous than extensive-range species. M. klissingiana starts with a narrow range (Tamaulipas only) and the simulation has it spreading to new habitats — shifting from narrow to extensive range. Consistent results mean genetic homogeneity should decrease.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — several other *Mammillaria* species could survive in Tamaulipas in the future.: Says nothing about M. klissingiana's genetic variation. Irrelevant to the finding being tested. ✗
  • Choice B — the genetic homogeneity of *M. klissingiana* decreased over time.: As M. klissingiana expanded to a wider range, the prior finding predicts less genetic homogeneity — exactly what this choice states. ✓
  • Choice C — there was a gradual increase in the genetic homogeneity of *Mammillaria* species in states neighboring Tamaulipas.: Talks about other species in neighboring states, not M. klissingiana's genetic change. Does not follow from the established pattern. ✗
  • Choice D — *Mammillaria* species other than *M. klissingiana* would become more common in Tamaulipas.: Addresses species abundance in Tamaulipas, not genetic variation of M. klissingiana. Irrelevant to the finding. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
